findings.title: Unauthenticated Stripe webhook forges any tenant's plan + billing_state (live, cross-tenant)
findings.failure_scenario: STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET is unset in the live deployment, so verifyStripeSignature is skipped (webhooks.ts:16 `if (webhookSecret)`). The route then applies the event to whatever tenant `metadata.tenantId`/`client_reference_id` names. PROVEN LIVE against a fresh demo tenant: an anonymous `POST /webhooks/stripe {type:'checkout.session.completed', data.object.metadata:{tenantId, plan:'scale'}}` returned `{applied:true, plan:'scale'}` and the tenant's account jumped to plan=scale, billingState=active, quota 60/18 — a free top-tier upgrade with no payment. The same vector forges `charge.dispute.created` (freeze any tenant = DoS), `customer.subscription.deleted` (force plan=free), or `invoice.payment_failed` (drive dunning) for ANY tenant whose UUID is known — and tenant UUIDs are exposed in the unauthenticated `/checkout/simulate?tenant=<id>` URL. billing state is fully attacker-writable.
findings.fix: Do not treat an unset secret as 'trusted'. In webhooks.ts, when STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET is absent, refuse to mutate state (return 503/no-op) instead of accepting unsigned events; make the secret an activation precondition. If a documented test-sim mode must accept unsigned events, gate it behind a config flag that is provably OFF in the live deployment, and add a test asserting an unsigned event is rejected when no secret is configured.

findings.title: Chargeback/dispute freeze is trivially bypassable via checkout — defeats the §12 protection
findings.failure_scenario: A disputed tenant has billing_state='disputed', and the tick's send-freeze guard (tick.ts:63) is the ONLY thing stopping sends. completeSimulatedCheckout (billing.ts:77) unconditionally runs `UPDATE tenant_profile SET plan=?, billing_state='active'` with no check on the prior state; so does the real checkout.session.completed handler (billing.ts:141) and customer.subscription.updated->active (billing.ts:163). PROVEN LIVE: froze a tenant via charge.dispute.created (billingState=disputed), then hit authed POST /checkout + the unauthenticated GET /checkout/simulate landing, and billingState flipped back to 'active' while the disputes row is still status='open'. The freeze the whole D5 chargeback lane exists to enforce is gone (sends resume) and the tenant is left in an inconsistent active+open-dispute state. Also fires without any deliberate re-checkout: a routine Stripe customer.subscription.updated with status active would lift the freeze.
findings.fix: Make 'disputed' (and 'canceled') a sticky state that checkout/subscription-active handlers must not overwrite: in completeSimulatedCheckout and the checkout.session.completed / subscription.updated handlers, guard the UPDATE with `WHERE billing_state NOT IN ('disputed','canceled')` (or read-and-branch), so only charge.dispute.closed(won) can lift a dispute freeze. Add a test that freezes a tenant then asserts a checkout completion leaves billing_state='disputed'.

findings.title: Cancel/re-subscribe/teardown leaks provisioned infra and locks out paying customers
findings.failure_scenario: teardown_records is a permanent tombstone used as the idempotency anchor (lifecycle.ts:95 `if (existing) return existing`), checkout has no lifecycle guard (startCheckout/completeSimulatedCheckout never clear it or block a torn-down tenant), and assertWithinProvisioningCap counts released/burning resources (quota.ts:43-48 do `COUNT(*) FROM domains/mailboxes` with no status filter). PROVEN LIVE: provisioned 2 domains on launch, cancelled (2 released, liability 2216c booked correctly), re-subscribed launch via checkout (billingState back to active but teardown record still present), then setup-infrastructure for 1 domain was REJECTED — 'plan launch allows at most 2 domains (have 2)' — a paying customer locked out because the released domains still count. Then upgraded to growth, provisioned 2 NEW domains, and a SECOND cancel returned alreadyCanceled=true with teardown.domainsReleased=2 (the OLD summary) — the 2 live domains/mailboxes are never released and never book liability. At activation that is a real registrar/mailbox vendor-spend leak (domains keep renewing, mailboxes keep billing) plus understated liability in the owner digest.
findings.fix: (1) In quota.ts count only live resources: `WHERE status='active'` for domains and `released_at IS NULL` for mailboxes. (2) Give the lifecycle a reactivation path: on re-checkout, clear/close the teardown_records row (or key teardown idempotency on a per-lifecycle epoch, not a permanent tombstone) so a subsequent cancel re-runs teardown on the new infra. (3) Alternatively block checkout entirely for a torn-down tenant until an explicit reactivate. Add tests: cancel->re-subscribe->provision succeeds within cap; cancel->re-subscribe->provision->cancel releases the new infra.

findings.title: Dunning-suspended (and won-dispute-after-suspend) tenants have no reactivation path — paid but permanently frozen
findings.failure_scenario: suspendTenant (ops-summary.ts:46-48) sets tenant_profile.status='suspended' after 4 failed payments (dunning). The tick freezes on status==='suspended' (tick.ts:63). But NO billing handler ever clears status back to 'active': customer.subscription.updated->active (billing.ts:163) and checkout.session.completed (billing.ts:141) write billing_state only; charge.dispute.closed(won) (billing.ts:219) writes billing_state only. So a customer who cures their past-due balance (payment succeeds) has billing_state='active' but status stays 'suspended' forever — they keep being billed by Stripe while every send is frozen, with no code path to recover. Lost revenue + a paying customer getting zero service.
findings.fix: On a billing-recovery transition (subscription.updated->active, checkout.session.completed, dispute won), clear a DUNNING-driven suspension: `UPDATE status='active' WHERE status='suspended'` — but only for dunning suspensions, not abuse TERMINATE. Distinguish them (e.g. a suspend_reason column, or gate on the D1 token still being live: terminate locks the token, dunning does not). Add a test: dunning-suspend then subscription.updated active -> tick sends again; terminate then any billing event -> stays suspended.

findings.title: Default end-of-period cancel tears down all infra immediately — 'effective:end_of_period' misrepresents actual behavior
findings.failure_scenario: POST /cancel defaults to immediate:false (routes/lifecycle.ts) => effective='end_of_period', billing_state='canceling'. But cancelTenant (lifecycle.ts:217) calls teardownTenant unconditionally, which immediately releases every domain+mailbox and pauses all campaigns (TeardownSummary comment: 'infra reclaim is always immediate'). So the DEFAULT cancel instantly kills the customer's service while the response claims service continues until period end. No Stripe cancel-at-period-end call is wired, so at activation the subscription auto-renews and keeps billing the customer for a period during which they have zero infra — paying for nothing. The returned effective='end_of_period' is a false promise to the caller/agent.
findings.fix: For effective='end_of_period', only flip billing_state='canceling' now and DEFER teardownTenant to period end (DO alarm / the D2 cron sweep), reclaiming infra when the paid period actually elapses; run immediate teardown only for immediate:true. Wire the Stripe cancel_at_period_end call at activation. Add a test asserting an end_of_period cancel leaves domains/mailboxes live until the period boundary.

findings.title: Tick send->bill sequence is non-atomic with no reaper — lost billing + orphaned 'sending' rows when real sends arm (B2)
findings.failure_scenario: The tick claims a row pending->sending (tick.ts:138), awaits email.send (tick.ts:144), then awaits billing.recordUsage (tick.ts:161). If the DO is evicted/crashes AFTER email.send commits but BEFORE recordUsage+the 'sent' commit, the email was sent but never billed, and the row is stuck status='sending' — the due-query only selects status='pending' (tick.ts:86), so nothing ever retries or reaps it. Harmless in sandbox (in-memory send, no real money), but when B2 arms real Inboxkit sends this is a lost-revenue + orphaned-state path. The code comment claiming 'a row is never left sent-but-unbilled' only covers the recordUsage-throws case, not eviction between send and bill.
findings.fix: Track a 'sending' reaper or make claim+send+bill a resumable saga step (B2): on tick start, re-drive rows stuck in 'sending' older than a threshold through the idempotent email.send + recordUsage keys (both already idempotency-keyed on row.id), then commit 'sent'. Fold into the B2 resumable-saga work rather than leaving the send loop's durability gap undocumented.
